name: Herbed Roast Leg of Lamb with Roast Onions&
recipeInstructions:
1. Use small red or white potatoes.
2. Insert slices of garlic (medium cloves, quartered) into slits made in the roast at about 3 inch intervals with a sharp knife. This adds a wonderful subtle flavor to the meat.
3. Mince the garlic clove and mash it to a paste with 1/4 tsp salt. Blanch onions in boiling water for 2 minutes, drain, and peel. Cut carrots into 1/2" pieces. Cook potatoes in boiling salted water for 10 minutes, drain, and halve.
4. In a small bowl whisk together the mustard, garlic paste, chopped rosemary, chopped thyme, soy sauce, white wine, and salt and pepper to taste. Add 2 tbsp of olive oil in a stream, whisking, until the mixture is well combined.
5. Brush the lamb generously on all sides with some of the mustard mixture and let it marinate in a lightly oiled roasting pan, covered and chilled for at least 6 hours or overnight.
6. Let the lamb come to room temperature and brush it with the reserved mustard mixture. In a bowl toss onions with 1 Tbsp of remaining oil, add onions and carrots to the pan, and roast them with the lamb in the middle of a preheated 450°F oven for 15 minutes.
7. In the bowl toss potatoes with the remaining 1 Tbsp oil and add them to the pan. Reduce heat to 350°F and roast the lamb and vegetables, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until a meat thermometer registers 140°F for medium-rare.
8. Transfer lamb to a large platter and let it stand for 20 minutes. Transfer onions and potatoes with a slotted spoon to a serving dish, leaving carrots in the pan, and keep them warm, covered.
9. Make the gravy: Pour off fat from the pan, add wine, and deglaze the pan over moderately high heat, scraping up brown bits. Boil mixture until reduced by half and strain through a sieve into a saucepan, pressing hard on carrots.
10. Add broth and bring to boil. In a small bowl knead together butter and flour until combined, then add to gravy a little at a time, whisking. Add juices from the lamb platter and salt and pepper to taste, simmer for 3 minutes or until thickened.
11. Spoon some potatoes and onions around the lamb and garnish with fresh thyme and rosemary sprigs. Serve carved lamb with gravy.
